ownerDecisionsRequired: Primary data store: Netlify Blobs vs managed PostgreSQL (new) -- highest priority, blocks persistence for nearly every function built so far

Pricing, discounts, deposits, payment timing (audit F030, F031) -- now directly blocks F026/F027/F028/F050/F052's real values; the ENGINES for all of these are done and tested, only the numbers are missing
Website Care Plan / Small Business IT plan limits and included work (audit F032) -- blocks F049/F052's real values and F021's fifth scoring factor
Customer account registration model: open vs invite-only (audit F033)
Data retention, deletion, backup-aging, legal-hold rules
Legal/privacy/consent wording, including open Critical audit findings F006 and F007

New paid providers/infrastructure generally -- also now includes: is Square staying the payment provider, or is a webhook-capable replacement being considered? F028's reconciliation design assumes Square remains the provider
Remote support tooling / device agents / automated website changes scope boundary
Missing individual F001-F060 function workbooks (new) -- also now the source of F020/F021's real configuration and F026/F050's real price sheet
filesRequiringReview: docs/development/ARCHITECTURE.md (target architecture proposal, frontend build-step recommendation)
knownRisks: No organization/tenant concept exists in the pre-Care-Hub codebase at all -- F001 is a from-scratch build, not an extension; src/domain/organization.js types exist but nothing persists them yet
Netlify Blobs has no secondary indexes; list()-scan pattern may not scale to Care Hub query needs regardless of which side of the data-store decision is taken
No payment webhook infrastructure exists at all -- F028's state machine is ready but has nothing to drive it until a provider integration exists

Customer documents/images are stored as base64 data URIs in Blobs, not object storage with signed URLs -- does not meet target private-file requirements at scale; src/domain/document.js models storageRef as opaque specifically to avoid repeating this

Two independent, near-duplicated single-file staff/customer console apps (admin.html, myaccount.html) with repeated auth/fetch boilerplate -- still not extracted (ARCHITECTURE.md's low-risk item, deferred until real Care Hub UI work begins)
F-numbering collision between audit findings (F001-F036) and Business Care Hub functions (F001-F060) -- mitigated via naming convention in DECISION_LOG.md

src/audit/blobsAuditSink.js and src/settings/blobsSettingsStore.js are written but not integration-tested against live Netlify Blobs (no credentials available in this environment) -- verify with a real smoke test once any endpoint calls them

src/policy/rbac.js's ROLE_CAPABILITIES map is a Session-1 transcription of the Roles & Permissions sheet's prose, not sourced from an individual F005 workbook (none exists yet, OWNER_DECISIONS.md #10) -- re-verify against the real workbook if/when provided
src/policy/fileValidation.js's size limits and MIME allowlist are engineering defaults, not an owner-approved policy -- exposed via F056 settings for adjustment, not hardcoded as final
src/policy/triageEngine.js and src/policy/priorityScoring.js are rule/weight INTERPRETERS with no real business configuration behind them yet
src/policy/ticketLifecycle.js's 14-day reopen window is an engineering default, not an owner-approved support-window policy

src/policy/pricingEngine.js and src/policy/entitlementCheck.js are, like the triage/priority engines, fully built and tested but functionally inert until Dylan supplies real prices/plan limits -- by Session 4's end, the majority of built code in this project is 'engine, not policy,' waiting on the same handful of owner decisions

None of Sessions 2-4's pure logic modules are wired to real data yet -- all tested against synthetic fixtures only; first real integration test happens once F001 persistence exists and an actual endpoint calls them
